hero.title: Free Online
hero.description: Rotate and flip your images with precision. 90°, 180° rotations and horizontal/vertical flips — all processed locally in your browser.

dropzone.orClickBrowse: or click to browse a file

preview.applyTransformationHint: Apply a transformation to enable download

items.title: Precise Rotation
items.description: Rotate images by 90° or 180° with pixel-perfect accuracy.

items.title: Mirror & Flip
items.description: Flip images horizontally or vertically to create mirror effects.

items.title: Instant Preview
items.description: See your changes immediately. No waiting for server processing.

howTo.title: How to Rotate & Flip Images Online
howTo.step1Desc: Drag any JPEG, PNG, or WebP file into the editor. It opens right in your browser with zero upload wait.
howTo.step2Desc: Pick a preset angle, toggle horizontal or vertical flip, or combine them. The preview updates in real time so you can see exactly what you get.
howTo.step3Desc: Choose an output format if you want to convert at the same time, then hit Download. The file is ready to use immediately.
howTo.tipText: Rotating by 90° or 180° keeps every pixel intact. Quality loss only happens during re-compression, so set the slider to 90%+ for visually lossless output.

why.title: Why Rotate & Flip Images?
why.p1: Cameras do not always get the orientation right. A phone might save a landscape shot as portrait, or the EXIF orientation tag gets lost during an export. A quick 90° rotation fixes the framing without touching the actual pixels, so there is no quality penalty beyond the final save step.
why.p2: Flipping is equally useful. Product photographers mirror images so that items face the same direction across a catalog grid. Designers flip selfie-mode screenshots to make text readable again. And anyone building a thumbnail sheet saves time by fixing orientation in the browser instead of opening a heavy desktop editor.
bullets: Fix sideways or upside-down photos caused by incorrect EXIF orientation tags.
Mirror selfies and product shots so text is readable and items face a consistent direction.
Make the correction right here in your browser — no need to install Photoshop or fire up GIMP for a simple rotation.

items.question: How do I rotate an image?
items.answer: Simply upload your image and use the rotation buttons to rotate left (90° counter-clockwise), right (90° clockwise), or 180°. The preview updates instantly.

items.question: What's the difference between flip and rotate?
items.answer: Rotating turns the image around its center point by a certain angle. Flipping creates a mirror image - horizontal flip mirrors left-to-right, vertical flip mirrors top-to-bottom.

items.question: Will rotating affect my image quality?
items.answer: Rotating by 90° increments is lossless for the pixel data. When saving as JPEG/WebP, the quality slider controls the compression level. PNG output is always lossless.

items.question: Can I combine rotation and flip?
items.answer: Yes! You can apply any combination of rotations and flips. Use the Reset button to return to the original orientation at any time.

items.question: Is my image uploaded to a server?
items.answer: No, all processing happens entirely in your browser using HTML5 Canvas. Your images never leave your device, ensuring complete privacy.
